Why CSS overflow: scroll Shows No Scrollbar on macOS Chrome (vs. Windows)

You set overflow: scroll, then realize Mac Chrome shows no scrollbar — but your coworker on Windows sees one fine. This post starts with the four overflow values, then explains macOS's Overlay Scrollbar mechanism — why Chrome on macOS doesn't always show a scrollbar even with overflow: scroll. Three fixes: ::-webkit-scrollbar to force it, the newer standard scrollbar-width and scrollbar-color, and flipping macOS's "Show scroll bars" system setting to "Always".
VSCode Opens Every Link in the Integrated Browser? How to Disable Auto Takeover

VSCode 1.109 shipped a new Integrated Browser, and once workbench.browser.openLocalhostLinks is on, every localhost link in the terminal or chat view gets pulled into the editor — tiny pane, painful to use. This post covers how to disable the two key settings (workbench.browser.openLocalhostLinks and simpleBrowser.useIntegratedBrowser), and how to still call the integrated browser manually from the command palette when you actually want it.
Chrome Keeps Caching Your Changes? Three Ways to Clear It Fast

You updated the page but Chrome keeps showing the old version. This post walks through three ways to clear Chrome's cache — keyboard shortcut, devtools, and an extension — plus a tip for how developers can avoid the problem in the first place.
A Mock API Skill Every Frontend Dev Should Have: Try Mockoon

Every frontend developer should know how to use a Mock API, whether you're senior or junior. This post covers why Mock APIs matter (so you're not stuck waiting on the backend), how to use Mockoon — my go-to Mock API tool — to set up environments, build routes from a spec, configure CORS and ports for frontend integration, and use Faker.js helpers for fake data.

VSCode is one of the most-used editors in the world and there are a ton of great extensions out there — but the sheer number can be overwhelming. People keep asking me which ones I use, so here's a list of mine.
